title: Sunk Cost Awareness

content.statement: Don't throw good effort after bad.
content.question: What are you continuing only because you've already invested, not because it works?
sop: Set a checkpoint date to evaluate any ongoing investment of time, money, or energy
Rate the future benefit and future cost, ignoring what's already spent (see calculation, page 6)
If the forward-looking number is negative, halt, regardless of how much has already gone in
Free up the capacity (time, money, attention) for something with a positive forward number
Write down the decision and why, so you don't quietly restart the same thing later
content.explainer: The money or time you've already spent is gone either way, it doesn't come back if you continue, and it doesn't get wasted retroactively if you stop. The only number that should actually drive the decision is what happens from here forward; anything else is just the discomfort of admitting the past investment didn't pan out.
questions: What are you continuing only because you've already invested, not because it works?
If you were starting today with zero history, would you still choose to keep doing this?
What's the actual future benefit of continuing, separate from how much you'd hate to admit the past investment didn't pay off?

definitions.def: Money or time already spent that can't be recovered.

content.equation_example: Future Benefit: rate 1-10 (10 = high forward value). Cost: rate 1-10 (ongoing cost to continue). Continue equals Future Benefit 3 minus Cost 7 equals -4, negative, meaning stop regardless of past investment. Future Benefit 8 minus Cost 3 equals 5, positive, meaning it's still worth continuing. A positive score means keep going based on what's ahead; a negative score means the past investment shouldn't buy the future another chance.
content.closing_statement: Only the road ahead should decide whether you continue.

caption:
Principle Nº 236: Sunk Cost Awareness

Don't throw good effort after bad. The money or time you've already spent is gone either way, it doesn't come back if you continue, and it doesn't get wasted retroactively if you stop.

Ask yourself: What are you continuing only because you've already invested, not because it works?
